meta.title: Geometric Invariants of the Autogenic Proto-Geometry
meta.description: Every physical constant as a geometric evaluation on D_IV^5. No series, no perturbation, no fitting.
meta.paper: Paper #83 — Geometric Invariants of the APG
meta.format: Each entry: symbol, name, BST formula, geometric source, value, observed, precision, theorem, toy, status

status_key.finite_sum: Finite sum over Bergman spectrum (<=137 terms)
status_key.series: Currently expressed as series, closed form unknown
status_key.missing: No BST expression yet — open problem
status_key.tension: BST value disagrees with observation beyond expected precision

next_priorities: W-15: Spectral zeta g-2 (full a_e as 137-term sum)
W-18: Individual quark masses (d,s,c,b), Lamb shift, hyperfine splitting
meta.inv4_audit: First pass complete. 4 tension flags resolved. T1437 corrected.
meta.inv4_phase2: W-54 observed values tightened for 7 entries
meta.elie_audit: Toy 1505: 45 tautological entries downgraded to structural, 3 confirmed as derived
meta.session_update: G-28 backfill: +24 entries (CKM matrix 10, gauge 5, leptons 5, quarks 4). Total now 1375.
meta.g29_update: G-29: +14 entries from gap domains (fluids 3, chemical_physics 4, observer_science 3, qft 2, electromagnetism 1, info_theory 1). 15 gap domains audited; 9 are structural/methodology (foundations, bst_physics, proof_complexity, linearization, classical_mech, quantum_foundations, quantum_mechanics, differential_geometry, optics) — no new numerical entries because they contain framework theorems, not predictions.
meta.g30_update: G-30: +4 entries (S_BH, sin(2beta), gravitino=N/A, Lambda running=0). 11/15 already in table. Honest catalog complete.

invariants.name: Uniqueness: Casimir = gauge Casimir
invariants.geometric_source: Algebraic condition

invariants.name: T186: Five Integers Uniqueness
invariants.geometric_source: The sun of the AC graph.

invariants.name: DM/baryon ratio
invariants.notes: Elie E-21: corrected from n_C+1/g=5.143 (4.1%) to 16/N_c=16/3=5.333 (0.58%). Constants JSON had correct value.

invariants.name: There is one geometry
invariants.geometric_source: The Autogenic Proto-Geometry. The unique bounded symmetric domain satisfying five simultaneous conditions. Everything derives from here.

invariants.name: Five integers determine everything
invariants.formula: rank=2, N_c=3, n_C=5, C₂=6, g=7 → N_max=137
invariants.geometric_source: The Cartan invariants of D_IV^5. Three independent (rank, N_c, n_C), two derived (C₂=rank·N_c, g=rank+n_C). Zero free parameters.

invariants.name: Grover speedup exponent
invariants.geometric_source: Quantum search speedup: N^(1/rank) = √N. Rank controls quantum advantage.

invariants.name: Quantum error threshold
invariants.geometric_source: Surface code threshold ≈ 1% ≈ α. The coupling constant IS the noise tolerance.

invariants.name: Non-linearizable fraction
invariants.geometric_source: Fraction of function space requiring depth 1 (Painlevé). The Gödel limit.

invariants.name: Linearizable fraction
invariants.geometric_source: Fraction of function space at depth 0. AC(0) completeness for this fraction.

invariants.name: BBN time as BST product
invariants.geometric_source: T1452: BBN onset = product of four sub-N_max integers. ALL four needed = all four thermally active. EXACT.

invariants.name: BST product 210 = C₂·g·n_C
invariants.geometric_source: Dark zone product. 210 = 2·3·5·7 = primorial(g). Casimir×genus×fiber. Physical meaning: TBD.

invariants.name: BST product 630 = C₂·N_c·g·n_C
invariants.geometric_source: All four odd primes × Casimir. 630 = rank·product(3,5,6,7) = 2·315. Physical meaning: TBD.

invariants.name: Cooperation threshold (numerical)
invariants.geometric_source: T583: Below f_crit, self-knowledge is free. Above, cooperation required.

invariants.name: Cooperation gap (numerical)
invariants.geometric_source: Gap between Gödel limit and cooperation threshold. Sociality forced by geometry.

invariants.name: Trust threshold
invariants.geometric_source: Fraction of consistent observations needed for trust. One deviation per N_max observations.

invariants.name: Punishment threshold (Hamilton)
invariants.geometric_source: Minimum relatedness for altruism. Hamilton's rule: r > c/b at r = 1/N_c.

invariants.name: Dictator game modal offer
invariants.geometric_source: Game theory: modal dictator offer ≈ 1/N_c. Observed 30-40% across cultures.

invariants.name: Ultimatum rejection threshold
invariants.geometric_source: Ultimatum game: offers below ~20% are rejected. f_crit = cooperation threshold.

invariants.name: Cowan's focused attention limit
invariants.geometric_source: Focused attention holds rank² = 4 items (Cowan 2001). vs Miller's g = 7.

invariants.name: Dunbar intimate circle
invariants.geometric_source: Closest social group = n_C. Support clique.

invariants.name: Dunbar sympathy group
invariants.geometric_source: Close friends = N_c·n_C. Sympathy group.

invariants.name: Dunbar band
invariants.geometric_source: Operational group = rank·n_C². Band.

invariants.name: Dunbar's number (BST)
invariants.geometric_source: Social group size = N_max + rank·C₂ = 137 + 12 = 149. Dunbar: ~150.

invariants.name: Dunbar megaband
invariants.geometric_source: Acquaintance network ≈ N_c·N_max. 421 vs observed ~500. 16% off.

invariants.name: Maximum learning rate
invariants.geometric_source: T318: CI coupling ≤ 19.1%. Maximum self-knowledge per observation cycle.

invariants.name: Defection strategy count
invariants.geometric_source: T581: Seven defection modes = genus. Each mode corresponds to one symmetry-breaking path.

invariants.name: Ebbinghaus forgetting exponent
invariants.geometric_source: Memory retention R = t^(-5/7). Observed Ebbinghaus exponent ≈ -0.7.

invariants.name: Heart chambers
invariants.geometric_source: Four chambers = spacetime dimension. — TAUTOLOGICAL: small-integer coincidence per Elie audit (Toy 1505). Not derived from D_IV^5.

invariants.name: ABO blood types
invariants.geometric_source: Four main types = rank². — TAUTOLOGICAL: small-integer coincidence per Elie audit (Toy 1505). Not derived from D_IV^5.

invariants.name: Rh factor types
invariants.geometric_source: Two Rh types = rank. — TAUTOLOGICAL: small-integer coincidence per Elie audit (Toy 1505). Not derived from D_IV^5.

invariants.name: Vital signs count
invariants.geometric_source: Five vital signs = compact dimension.

invariants.name: Cervical vertebrae
invariants.geometric_source: Seven cervical vertebrae in nearly all mammals = genus.

invariants.name: Trophic levels (typical)
invariants.geometric_source: Four trophic levels = spacetime.

invariants.name: Food web connectance
invariants.geometric_source: Typical C ≈ 1/n_C to 1/N_c for food webs.
invariants.notes: [Reclassified I→S 2026-05-15 by Grace. Connectance ranges 0.1-0.3, identified with 1/g to 1/n_C. Structural pattern in ecology, material-specific.]

invariants.name: Most common vowel count
invariants.geometric_source: Five vowels in most languages = compact dimension.

invariants.name: Consonant articulation places
invariants.formula: g = 7 (bilabial, labiodental, dental, alveolar, postalveolar, velar, glottal)
invariants.geometric_source: Seven places of articulation = genus.

invariants.name: IPv4 address octets
invariants.geometric_source: Four octets × rank³ bits = 32-bit address.

invariants.name: IPv4 address bits
invariants.geometric_source: 32 bits = rank⁵.

invariants.name: IPv6 address bits
invariants.geometric_source: 128 bits = rank^g = GF(128).

invariants.name: TCP/IP layer count
invariants.geometric_source: Four layers = spacetime.

invariants.name: OSI model layers
invariants.geometric_source: Seven OSI layers = genus.

invariants.name: USB major versions
invariants.geometric_source: Four major USB versions.

invariants.name: Water density max temperature
invariants.geometric_source: Maximum density at rank²°C. Anomalous.

invariants.name: Neutral pH
invariants.geometric_source: pH 7 = genus. Neutral = self-conjugate.

invariants.name: Manganese max oxidation state
invariants.geometric_source: Mn has max oxidation g = +7.

invariants.name: Noble gas group number
invariants.geometric_source: Group 18 = 2·9 = rank·N_c².

invariants.name: Solar system planets
invariants.geometric_source: Eight planets = rank³ (post-Pluto).

invariants.name: Lagrange points
invariants.geometric_source: Five Lagrange points = compact dimension.

invariants.name: Titius-Bode doubling
invariants.geometric_source: Each orbit ≈ rank × previous.

invariants.name: Laws of thermodynamics
invariants.geometric_source: Four laws = spacetime.

invariants.name: Gibbs phase rule
invariants.geometric_source: F = C - P + 2. The rank in Gibbs.

invariants.name: FFT butterfly operations
invariants.geometric_source: Each butterfly combines rank = 2 inputs. — TAUTOLOGICAL: small-integer coincidence per Elie audit (Toy 1505). Not derived from D_IV^5.

invariants.name: Fourier transform pairs
invariants.geometric_source: Two conjugate domains = rank. — TAUTOLOGICAL: small-integer coincidence per Elie audit (Toy 1505). Not derived from D_IV^5.

invariants.name: Heisenberg uncertainty
invariants.geometric_source: ΔxΔp ≥ ℏ/rank. The rank in uncertainty.

invariants.name: Arrow's theorem minimum
invariants.geometric_source: Arrow: no fair voting for ≥ N_c = 3 candidates with ≥ rank voters.

invariants.name: Condorcet paradox minimum
invariants.geometric_source: Cyclic preferences possible with N_c candidates.

invariants.name: Cayley-Hamilton degree
invariants.geometric_source: Every matrix satisfies its own char poly of degree n. Self-reference at depth 1.

invariants.name: Noether theorem structure
invariants.geometric_source: One-to-one. Same rank = 2 as fiber count.

invariants.name: SM gauge group total rank
invariants.geometric_source: SU(3) rank 2 + SU(2) rank 1 + U(1) rank 1 = 4 = rank².

invariants.name: Quartic equation solvable
invariants.geometric_source: Galois: polynomials solvable by radicals iff degree ≤ 4 = rank². A₅ simple at n_C = 5 blocks quintics.

invariants.name: Quintic equation unsolvable
invariants.geometric_source: Abel-Ruffini: degree n_C = 5 is the first unsolvable. A_{n_C} = A₅ is simple.

invariants.name: Euler's identity terms
invariants.geometric_source: Five fundamental constants = n_C. e^(iπ) + 1 = 0.

invariants.name: Hardy-Ramanujan number
invariants.geometric_source: 1729 = g·(2C₂+1)·Q = 7·13·19. Three BST products.

invariants.name: First BST digits of π
invariants.geometric_source: First 6 digits of π contain four BST integers. COINCIDENCE — labeled honestly.
invariants.notes: [2026-05-15 Grace C-tier sweep: reclassify C→S, precision band structural. C-tier label assumed conjecture-dependent derivation but item is structural/>2% — S-tier honest.]

invariants.name: Fibonacci-BST closure
invariants.formula: F₂=1, F₃=rank, F₄=N_c, F₅=n_C, F₆=rank³, F₇=13=2C₂+1, F₈=N_c·g=21
invariants.geometric_source: Fibonacci numbers hit BST integers at F₃ through F₈. After F₈, they outgrow.
invariants.canonical_form: F₂=1, F₃=rank, F₄=N_c, F₅=n_C, F₆=rank³, F₇=13=2rank·N_c+1, F₈=N_c·(rank+n_C)=21

invariants.name: First two perfect numbers
invariants.geometric_source: 6 and 28 are the first two perfect numbers = C₂ and rank²·g.

invariants.name: 1729 = g·(2C₂+1)·Q
invariants.geometric_source: Hardy-Ramanujan taxicab. All three factors are BST: g, 2C₂+1, Q.

invariants.name: π continued fraction [3;7,15,1,292,...]
invariants.geometric_source: π = [3; 7, 15, ...]. First two convergents: N_c and g. Third: N_c·n_C.

invariants.name: e continued fraction [2;1,2,1,1,4,1,1,6,...]
invariants.geometric_source: e = [rank; 1, rank, 1, 1, rank², ...]. Pattern involves rank and its powers.
invariants.notes: [2026-05-15 Grace C-tier sweep: reclassify C→S, precision band structural. C-tier label assumed conjecture-dependent derivation but item is structural/>2% — S-tier honest.]

invariants.name: Wallis product for π
invariants.geometric_source: π/2 = (2·2)/(1·3) × (4·4)/(3·5) × ... Rank products over adjacent BST integers.

invariants.name: Γ(1/rank) = √π
invariants.geometric_source: Gamma at 1/rank = √π. The rank connects Γ to π.

invariants.name: 2-SAT threshold
invariants.geometric_source: 2-SAT solvable in poly time. Threshold at α = 1.

invariants.name: 4-SAT threshold
invariants.geometric_source: 4-SAT threshold. rank⁴·ln(rank) with corrections.

invariants.name: Parity circuit depth lower bound
invariants.geometric_source: Parity not in AC⁰. Requires depth Ω(log n). BST: depth > 1 = nonlinear.

invariants.name: NC parallel depth
invariants.geometric_source: NC = polylog depth. BST: linearizable fraction = n_C/C₂.

invariants.name: Equality randomized CC
invariants.geometric_source: Fingerprinting: O(1) = O(rank/rank). Shared randomness eliminates cost.

invariants.name: Disjointness deterministic CC
invariants.geometric_source: Disjointness requires Ω(n) bits. Linear in input size.

invariants.name: BQP ⊆ PSPACE
invariants.geometric_source: Quantum doesn't break space bounds. Rank helps time, not space.

invariants.name: Quantum sampling advantage
invariants.geometric_source: Quantum sampling: 2^n states from n qubits. rank^n.

invariants.name: Graph coloring NP-complete
invariants.geometric_source: k-coloring NP-complete for k ≥ N_c = 3. Color charge = complexity threshold.

invariants.name: 2-coloring polynomial
invariants.geometric_source: Bipartiteness check = rank-coloring = polynomial. Below N_c threshold.

invariants.name: Shannon source coding
invariants.geometric_source: Minimum encoding = entropy. BST: H measured in rank-ary units.

invariants.name: Shannon channel capacity
invariants.geometric_source: Maximum reliable rate = capacity. BST: capacity involves rank.

invariants.name: Public goods MPCR threshold
invariants.geometric_source: Cooperation collapses when marginal return < 1/n_C. Compact fiber sets the cooperation floor.

invariants.name: Free-rider equilibrium fraction
invariants.geometric_source: Painlevé fraction = nonlinear = defection. The noncooperative fraction IS the Gödel limit.

invariants.name: Commons collapse threshold
invariants.geometric_source: Tragedy of the commons: collapse when users exceed spectral cap / Casimir ≈ 23. Same as Chandrasekhar scale separation.

invariants.name: Small-world transition probability
invariants.geometric_source: Watts-Strogatz: small-world transition at p ≈ 1/N. BST: p = α for N_max-scale.

invariants.name: Scale-free network exponent
invariants.geometric_source: Barabási-Albert: degree distribution P(k) ∝ k^(-N_c). Color charge controls network topology.

invariants.name: Six degrees of separation
invariants.geometric_source: Milgram (1967): average social path length ≈ C₂ = 6. The Casimir IS the social diameter.

invariants.name: Social network clustering
invariants.geometric_source: Typical social clustering ≈ 1/g. The genus inverse controls local connectivity.

invariants.name: Loss aversion ratio
invariants.geometric_source: Kahneman-Tversky: losses weighted rank + 1/rank² = 2.25 vs gains. rank = the doubling, 1/rank² = curvature correction.

invariants.name: Anchoring bias weight
invariants.geometric_source: Tversky-Kahneman: anchor gets ~50% = 1/rank weight. The rank controls the balance between anchor and adjustment.

invariants.name: Calibration curve inflection
invariants.geometric_source: Well-calibrated at ~80% = linearizable fraction. Above 83%: overconfidence. The Painlevé boundary of judgment.

invariants.name: r/K selection transition
invariants.geometric_source: r-strategists below n_C offspring investment, K-strategists above. Compact fiber = the parental investment threshold.

invariants.name: Optimal foraging exponent
invariants.geometric_source: Marginal value theorem: stay time ∝ (travel)^(1/rank). Square root from rank.

invariants.name: Hick's law logarithm base
invariants.geometric_source: RT = a + b·log_rank(n). Reaction time grows as log base rank of choices.

invariants.name: Weber-Fechner just-noticeable difference
invariants.geometric_source: Perceived intensity ∝ ln(stimulus). Logarithmic perception = depth-0 counting.

invariants.name: Subitizing limit
invariants.geometric_source: Instant number recognition ≤ rank² = 4 items. Above 4: counting required. Spacetime dimension = instant perception limit.

invariants.name: Chromatic polynomial P(K₃, N_c)
invariants.geometric_source: Triangle chromatic polynomial at color charge = Casimir. Graph theory meets QCD.

invariants.name: Chromatic polynomial P(K₅, n_C)
invariants.geometric_source: Complete graph K₅ at fiber dimension = 120 = dominant L1 correction denominator = |S₅| = |A₅|×rank.

invariants.name: Kneser graph χ(K(n_C,rank))
invariants.geometric_source: Kneser graph on BST integers: χ = N_c. Lovász theta confirms. Color charge = Kneser chromatic.

invariants.name: Ramsey number R(3,3)
invariants.geometric_source: R(3,3) = 6. Ramsey at color charge = Casimir. Minimum guaranteed monochromatic triangle in C₂-complete graph.

invariants.name: N_c = universal hardness threshold
invariants.formula: k-coloring NP-complete at k = N_c = 3, polynomial at k = rank = 2
invariants.geometric_source: BRIDGE: QCD confinement (N_c colors) = computational hardness (N_c-coloring NP-complete). Same integer, same threshold, different physics.

invariants.name: Unit gap g - C₂ = 1
invariants.geometric_source: The gap between genus and Casimir is exactly 1. This forces n_C = 5 (new uniqueness route from T1455). Also: N_c-coloring is NP-hard, (N_c+1)-coloring = rank²-coloring suffices for planar. The gap between hardness and sufficiency is g-C₂ = 1.

invariants.name: Four-Color as N_c + 1 sufficiency
invariants.geometric_source: Four-Color Theorem: rank² colors suffice for planar. N_c colors are insufficient (K₄ needs 4). The chromatic gap N_c → N_c+1 = rank² is the computational transition from hard to solved.

invariants.name: N_c: confinement ↔ coloring ↔ Ramsey
invariants.formula: N_c = 3: (1) QCD confinement, (2) graph coloring NP-complete, (3) R(3,3) = C₂, (4) A_N_c last solvable
invariants.geometric_source: BRIDGE: Four independent appearances of N_c as a threshold. QCD confines at N_c colors. Coloring becomes hard at N_c. Ramsey at (N_c,N_c) = C₂. A_{N_c} = A₃ is the last solvable alternating group.

TEST: TESTABLE: Next-gen direct detection (XLZD, DARWIN) should find nothing. BST predicts null result.

invariants.geometric_source: Elie Toy 1691. The cyclotomic factors carry +1 (RFC adds to each factor). Overall product carries -1 (observer subtracts from count). Vacuum subtraction in number-theoretic dress.

invariants.formula: lambda_k = k(k+n_C) invariant under k -> -(k+n_C). P(k) is degree 5 (odd), so P(-(k+n_C)) = -P(k). Weyl group action.

invariants.formula: H(t) = (1+t)/(1-t)^6. Denominator exponent = C_2 = spectral dimension. (1+t) factor from rank = 2.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1708 (6/8). The Hilbert series encodes BOTH spectral dimension (C_2) and multiplicity (rank) in its two factors.

invariants.formula: L=1: {rank}. L=2: {rank,N_c}. L=3: {rank,N_c,n_C}. Each loop introduces the next BST prime into the denominator set.

invariants.name: Sum of spectral zeta residues = g/24 = 7/24
invariants.formula: Res[1] + Res[2] + Res[3] = 1/5 + 1/12 + 1/120 = 7/24. The genus appears as the sum.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1770. The sum of all three residues = g/24 = g/(lambda_3). Genus over QCD eigenvalue.

invariants.name: 13 = F_7: the Thirteen Theorem value is the 7th Fibonacci
invariants.formula: g + C_2 = 13 = F_7. Also F_7 = F_g. The seventh Fibonacci IS the genus-th Fibonacci.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1770. The Thirteen Theorem's value is itself a Fibonacci number at the genus index.

invariants.name: Residue of zeta_B at s=3
invariants.geometric_source: Inverse Plancherel measure at k=0

invariants.name: Residue of zeta_B at s=2
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral zeta pole structure of D_IV^5

invariants.name: Residue of zeta_B at s=1
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral zeta pole structure of D_IV^5

invariants.name: Sum of all residues of zeta_B
invariants.geometric_source: Total spectral weight of D_IV^5

invariants.name: Functional equation center
invariants.geometric_source: Curvature shift reduces FE center by rank

invariants.name: Short root multiplicity of B_2
invariants.geometric_source: Root system of D_IV^5 = SO_0(5,2)/[SO(5)xSO(2)]

invariants.name: FE rational prefactor involution
invariants.geometric_source: Algebraic identity for P(s)=(s-4)(s-5)/[(s-1)(s-2)]

invariants.name: FE transcendental part involution
invariants.geometric_source: Consequence of R*R=1 and P*P=1

invariants.name: Phi at FE center
invariants.geometric_source: Antisymmetric fixed point of FE

invariants.name: Phi at rho_2 = N_c/rank
invariants.geometric_source: Mellin transform of heat kernel trace

invariants.geometric_source: Mellin transform of heat kernel trace
invariants.notes: [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, 1-2%]

invariants.name: Spectral zeta convergence rate
invariants.geometric_source: Hurwitz expansion of Bergman spectral zeta

invariants.name: P(0) = dim_R(D_IV^5)
invariants.geometric_source: Rational prefactor at FE boundary

invariants.name: P(N_c/rank) = -n_C*g
invariants.geometric_source: Rational prefactor at rho_2

invariants.name: Spectral zeta derivative at s=0
invariants.geometric_source: Bergman spectral zeta on D_IV^5

invariants.name: Spectral determinant of D_IV^5
invariants.geometric_source: Regularized product of Bergman eigenvalues

invariants.name: Part B of spectral zeta derivative
invariants.formula: C_log2*log(2) + C_log3*log(3) + C_log5*log(5) where C_log2=C_log3=0, C_log5=1
invariants.geometric_source: Finite log sum from d_k antisymmetry

invariants.name: log(2) coefficient cancels in zeta_B'(0)
invariants.geometric_source: d_k antisymmetry: d_k = -d_{-5-k}

invariants.name: log(3) coefficient cancels in zeta_B'(0)
invariants.geometric_source: d_k antisymmetry: d_k = -d_{-5-k}

invariants.name: log(2),log(3),log(4) cancel — only log(n_C) survives in zeta_B'(0)
invariants.formula: d_k has zeros at k=-1,-2,-3,-4. d(m-5)=0 for m=1,2,3,4. Only m=n_C=5 survives: d(0)=1. Ghost spectrum kills all logs except log(n_C).
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1778. The Hilbert function has n_C-1=4 ghost zeros that select n_C. Structural, not numerical.

invariants.name: det'(Delta) ≈ N_c^2/(rank^2*n_C) = 9/20 at 0.008%
invariants.formula: det'(Delta) = exp(-zeta_B'(0)) ~ 9/20 at 0.008%. NOT exact (Toy 1781). Gap = 7.66e-5 involves Glaisher-Kinkelin constant.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1778. Spectral determinant = N_c^2/(rank^2*n_C).

invariants.name: zeta_B'(0) = log(n_C) + 2*[149/60*zR'(-1) + zR'(-3) + 1/60*zR'(-5)]
invariants.formula: Exact formula. 149 = N_max+rank*C_2. 60 = n_C!/rank. No quadrature needed.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1778/1779. Part B = log(n_C) EXACT. Part A ≈ log(4/9) at 0.01%.

invariants.name: Phi(3/2) ≈ N_max/(N_max+g) = 137/144 at 0.17%
invariants.formula: Phi at s=N_c/rank = 3/2: value ≈ 137/144 = N_max/(N_max+g). The fine structure constant appears in the FE!
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1774 (10/10). Corrected Mellin values.

invariants.name: Phi(3) = -1: antisymmetric fixed point at FE center
invariants.formula: Phi(s)*Phi(6-s) = 1. At s=3 (center): Phi(3)^2 = 1, Phi(3) = -1. The FE center is antisymmetric.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1774. zeta_B changes sign under FE map. Odd L-function behavior.

invariants.name: P(s)*P(6-s) = 1: rational prefactor is self-inverse
invariants.formula: P(s) = (s-4)(s-5)/[(s-1)(s-2)]. P(s)*P(6-s) = 1 algebraically. The rational FE is its own inverse.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1773 (9/10). The ENTIRE non-rational part Phi(s)*Phi(6-s) = 1 too.

invariants.name: Gamma(g/rank) = N_c*n_C*sqrt(pi)/rank^N_c = 15*sqrt(pi)/8
invariants.formula: Gamma(7/2) = (5/2)(3/2)(1/2)*Gamma(1/2) = 15*sqrt(pi)/8. BST identity in the Gamma function.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1776. Pochhammer: (n_C/rank)(N_c/rank)(1/rank)*sqrt(pi).

invariants.name: Cheeger constant of D_IV^5: h = sqrt(34)/rank = sqrt(g+N_c^3)/rank
invariants.formula: h = |rho| = sqrt((n_C/rank)^2 + (N_c/rank)^2) = sqrt(34)/2. Continuum threshold = |rho|^2 = 34/4 = 8.5. First discrete eigenvalue lambda_1 = C_2 = 6 sits BELOW continuum by Wallach gap n_C/rank = 5/2.
invariants.geometric_source: Cheeger-Buser inequality with known BST spectrum. h^2 = 17 = the neutrino seesaw number. The Cheeger bottleneck IS the confinement width.
invariants.notes: CORRECTED: h gives Cheeger lower bound. The key physics: lambda_1 < |rho|^2 means mass gap is discrete series (bound state). Gap to continuum = n_C/rank = Wallach point.

invariants.name: Cheeger-Buser: h^2/4 = 17/8 ≤ lambda_1 = C_2 = 6
invariants.formula: Cheeger lower: h^2/4 = 34/16 = 2.125 ≤ lambda_1 = C_2 = 6. HOLDS. Buser upper 2h ≤ lambda_1 does NOT apply (negative Ricci). Continuous spectrum threshold |rho|^2 = 34/4 = 8.5 > lambda_1 = 6 → mass gap in discrete series.
invariants.geometric_source: The spectral gap C_2 = 6 bounds the confinement bottleneck from above. Mass gap is large BECAUSE geometry is hard to cut.
invariants.notes: CORRECTED: Buser upper bound requires Ricci ≥ 0, which D_IV^5 violates (non-compact). Lower bound holds. lambda_1 = 6 < |rho|^2 = 8.5 means mass gap is discrete series, below continuous spectrum.

invariants.name: Cheeger bottleneck ≈ N_c: confinement IS the minimum geometric cut
invariants.formula: h = sqrt(34)/2 ≈ 2.915 ≈ N_c = 3. Disconnecting D_IV^5 requires cutting through N_c color channels. Confinement = Cheeger bottleneck.
invariants.geometric_source: Cheeger constant ≈ N_c connects: spectral gap (C_2), confinement (Hamming N_c), neutrino seesaw (1/34), and the g+N_c^3 identity chain — all through one geometric inequality.
invariants.notes: Confinement = Cheeger bottleneck ≈ N_c. PLUS: bound states embedded in continuum above lambda = 8.5 are topologically protected (discrete series). Confinement is not just trapping — it is dissolution prevention.

invariants.name: Cheeger h^2 = 17 = neutrino seesaw number
invariants.formula: h^2 = 34/rank = 17 = N_c*C_2 - 1 (RFC on 18 seesaw modes). The SAME 17 in: seesaw denominator 34 = rank*17, Debye Au = rank*n_C*17, YBCO/Al ~ 79 ≈ rank^4*n_C - 1.
invariants.geometric_source: The Cheeger constant squared connects to the neutrino sector through the same RFC integer 17. Geometry (Cheeger) = particle physics (seesaw) via one number.

invariants.name: Wallach gap n_C/rank = 5/2 = stability margin for mass gap
invariants.formula: lambda_1 = C_2 = 6. Continuum at |rho|^2 = 34/4 = 8.5. Gap = 8.5 - 6 = 2.5 = n_C/rank = Wallach point.
invariants.geometric_source: The distance from the mass gap to the continuous spectrum IS the Wallach parameter. Matter is stable because this gap is large enough. n_C = 5 forces Wallach = 5/2.
invariants.notes: WHY matter is stable: Wallach gap prevents mass gap from dissolving into continuum. If n_C < 5 (e.g. n_C=4 → gap=1.5), bound states would be less stable.

invariants.name: Continuous spectrum threshold |rho|^2 = 34/4 = 8.5
invariants.formula: |rho|^2 = (n_C/rank)^2 + (N_c/rank)^2 = 25/4 + 9/4 = 34/4. Scattering states begin at 8.5. Discrete series below.
invariants.geometric_source: On non-compact D_IV^5, the L^2 spectrum has discrete series (bound states) below |rho|^2 and principal series (scattering) above. 34 = g + N_c^3.

invariants.name: Higher eigenvalues embedded in continuum but topologically protected
invariants.formula: lambda_k > |rho|^2 = 8.5 for k >= 2 (lambda_2 = 14). These discrete series reps are EMBEDDED in the continuum but don't dissolve. Protected by representation theory.
invariants.geometric_source: The proton is a bound state embedded in the scattering continuum. It doesn't dissolve because discrete series representations are topologically protected. Error correction: Hamming distance N_c = 3 prevents decay into continuum.
invariants.notes: Confinement is not just trapping. It is bound states refusing to dissolve into the continuum even when they have enough energy. Topological protection = error correction.

invariants.name: D_IV^5 intrinsically mixed: discrete + continuous spectra overlap
invariants.formula: Discrete series (bound states, particles) and principal series (scattering, communication) coexist above |rho|^2 = 8.5. Can't separate them — both needed for consistent physics.
invariants.geometric_source: The geometry can't be cleanly separated. Discrete = recorded information (matter). Continuous = communication channel (scattering). Wallach gap = noise margin keeping them from interfering.
invariants.notes: Casey's substrate: S^2 records (discrete), S^1 communicates (continuous). Wallach gap = margin between recording and communication. Both are needed.

invariants.name: Uniqueness → Wallach → stable matter: 2^(n-2)=n+3 → n_C=5 → gap=5/2
invariants.formula: 2^(n-2)=n+3 forces n_C=5. Wallach = n_C/rank = 5/2 = 2.5. This is large enough for stable bound states. If n_C=4: gap=2.0 (marginal). If n_C=3: gap=1.5 (unstable).
invariants.geometric_source: The universe has stable matter BECAUSE the uniqueness equation gives n_C=5, which gives a Wallach gap of 5/2. One equation → stable atoms.
invariants.notes: Chain: 2^(n-2)=n+3 → n=5 → Wallach=5/2 → mass gap stable → bound states protected → matter exists → observers possible. One equation, stable universe.

invariants.name: Leading Hurwitz coefficient a_1 = (N_max + rank*C_2)/(n_C!/rank)
invariants.formula: a_1 = 149/60 = (137 + 2*6)/(120/2). Glaisher-Kinkelin coefficient is a_1/6 = 149/360 = (N_max + rank*C_2)/(C_2*(n_C!/rank))
invariants.geometric_source: Hilbert function leading coefficient in three-stream Hurwitz decomposition of zeta_B(s)

invariants.name: n_C selection theorem: only log(n_C) survives in spectral determinant
invariants.formula: For Q^n Bergman zeta, d_k has zeros at k=-1,...,-(n-1). Log coefficient of m is d(m-n), which vanishes for m=1,...,n-1. Only m=n gives d(0)=1. Hence Part B = log(n).
invariants.geometric_source: Algebraic geometry of Hilbert function on quadric hypersurface Q^n

invariants.name: Spectral determinant gap: fine cancellation of O(1) terms
invariants.formula: Delta = Part_A - log(4/9) = 7.66e-5. Three O(1) terms: rational 149/360, Glaisher -(149/30)*log(A_GK), higher 2*zR'(-3)+(1/30)*zR'(-5) cancel to O(10^-5).
invariants.geometric_source: Glaisher-Kinkelin + Stieltjes constants from Bergman spectral decomposition
invariants.observed: N/A — gap in spectral determinant match
invariants.notes: [2026-05-15 Grace: I→S: Δ = 7.66e-5 between Part_A and log(4/9), mathematical observation about three O(1) terms.]

invariants.name: 149 = N_max + rank*C_2 = 137 + 12
invariants.formula: Leading Hurwitz numerator 149 = N_max + rank*C_2. Also: 149 is prime. 149/30 = (N_max+rank*C_2)/(n_C*C_2).
invariants.geometric_source: Hilbert function expansion on D_IV^5

invariants.notes: Gap between first discrete eigenvalue (C_2=6) and continuous spectrum (|rho|^2=8.5). Equals Wallach parameter. Stability margin for bound states.
Continuous spectrum threshold. 34 = rank*17 = g + N_c^3. 4*|rho|^2 = 34 connects to seesaw.

invariants.name: Heckman-Opdam c-function = c_reg(s) for B_2(3,1)
invariants.formula: c_reg(s) = Gamma(s)/Gamma(s+3/2) * [Gamma(s)/Gamma(s+1/2)]^2. Three Gamma ratios from three non-degenerate positive roots of B_2.
invariants.geometric_source: Heckman-Opdam theory for B_2 root system with multiplicities (m_short, m_long) = (N_c, 1) = (3, 1). Citation: Opdam (1995), Acta Math. 175.
invariants.notes: Toy 1783 (25/25). Lyra's c_reg (Toy 1752) IS the Heckman-Opdam c-function. Not a new result — a citation.

invariants.name: Hilbert function factored along B_2 roots
invariants.formula: P(k) = m*(m^2-1/4)*(m^2-9/4)/60 where m=k+n_C/2. Factors: m^2-1/4 = lambda+C_2 (long roots), m^2-9/4 = lambda+rank^2 (short roots), 60=n_C!/rank.
invariants.geometric_source: Root system B_2(3,1). Short roots shift by N_c/rank = 3/2, long roots by 1/rank = 1/2. Sum of shifts = rho_1 = n_C/rank.
invariants.notes: Toy 1783. The degeneracy function encodes root multiplicities. Five linear factors, one per positive root (including conjugates).

invariants.name: c-function two-root decomposition (Casey directive)
invariants.formula: c_reg = c_short * c_long. c_short(s) = Gamma(s)/Gamma(s+N_c/rank). c_long(s) = [Gamma(s)/Gamma(s+1/rank)]^2. Factorizes along B_2 root types.
invariants.geometric_source: Standard Heckman-Opdam factorization for B_2. Casey's two-root FE directive (April 30) is the root-type decomposition.
invariants.notes: Toy 1783. Validated Casey's insight: full FE = product of two rank-1 FEs. c_short = color sector, c_long = rank sector.

invariants.name: P(k)/|c|^{-2} asymptotic ratio = n_C!/rank = 60
invariants.formula: P(k) / |c_reg(k+5/2)|^{-2} -> n_C!/rank = 60 as k -> infinity. P = Weyl dimension (polynomial), |c|^{-2} = Plancherel density (transcendental). Same roots, different representations.
invariants.geometric_source: P and |c|^{-2} both encode B_2(3,1) root structure. Polynomial vs Gamma factorizations of the same data. Not proportional, but structurally linked.
invariants.notes: Toy 1783. HONEST: P(k) is NOT proportional to |c|^{-2}. They share root structure but differ in representation (Weyl vs Harish-Chandra). [2026-05-15 Grace: I→S: c_reg asymptotic behavior is mathematical statement about c-function structure, not numerical observable.]

invariants.name: Wallach gap = n*(n-4)/2 for Q^n, equals n_C/rank = 5/2 at n=5
invariants.geometric_source: Spectrum of Laplacian on D_IV^5: lambda_1 = C_2 = 6, |rho|^2 = 34/4 = 8.5, gap = 2.5

invariants.name: Energy per bit-flip = n_C/C_2 = 5/6
invariants.formula: Wallach gap / Hamming distance = (n_C/rank) / N_c = n_C/(rank*N_c) = n_C/C_2 = 5/6
invariants.geometric_source: Wallach gap divided by error correction distance

invariants.name: lambda_1/|rho|^2 = C_2/(34/4) = 12/17
invariants.formula: First eigenvalue over continuum threshold = 6/(17/2) = 12/17. Gap fraction = 1 - 12/17 = 5/17.
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral gap ratio on D_IV^5

invariants.name: Stability: n_C = 5 unique satisfying 2^(n-2)=n+3 AND n*(n-4)/2 > 0
invariants.formula: Hamming constraint 2^(n-2)=n+3 has unique solution n=5. Wallach stability n*(n-4)/2 > 0 requires n > 4. Combined: n=5 uniquely.
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral stability + error correction on quadric hypersurface

invariants.name: zeta_B(C_2)/zeta_B(g) = 439/72
invariants.geometric_source: Ratio of spectral zeta at s=C_2 and s=g on D_IV^5

invariants.name: 439 = C_2^3*rank + g (prime)
invariants.formula: 439 = 6^3 * 2 + 7 = C_2^3*rank + g. Same form as N_max = N_c^3*n_C + rank = 137.
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral zeta continued fraction convergent on D_IV^5

invariants.name: zeta_B(g/rank) = 1/g^2 = 1/49
invariants.formula: Spectral zeta at s=g/rank=7/2 equals 1/g^2. Emergent: k=1 contributes only 65%.
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral zeta at half-genus evaluation on D_IV^5

invariants.name: zeta_B(4)*N_max^2 = n_C^3 = 125
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral zeta at first convergent integer on D_IV^5

invariants.name: zB(4)*zB(6)/[zB(5)*zB(7)] = C_2*g = 42
invariants.formula: Cross ratio of spectral zeta at four consecutive BST integers. Leading = C_2^2 = 36, excess = C_2, total = C_2*(C_2+1) = C_2*g.
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral zeta cross-ratio on D_IV^5

invariants.name: N_c*zB(4) + rank*zB(8) = 1/(n_C^2*rank) = 1/50
invariants.formula: BST-weighted sum of spectral zeta values equals 1/(n_C^2*rank).
invariants.geometric_source: Spectral zeta linear combination on D_IV^5

invariants.name: 1st-order eigenvalue deviation ratio -> g/N_c = 7/3
invariants.formula: R(s) = zB(s)/zB(s+1). delta(s) = R(s)-C_2. delta(s)/delta(s+1) -> lambda_2/lambda_1 = 14/6 = g/N_c as s -> inf.
invariants.geometric_source: Eigenvalue hierarchy of Laplacian on D_IV^5

invariants.name: 2nd-order deviation ratio -> C_2*rank/g = 12/7
invariants.formula: Second-order correction to R(s)-C_2 converges to 12/7, NOT lambda_3/lambda_1 = 4.
invariants.geometric_source: Eigenvalue hierarchy on D_IV^5

invariants.name: Leading spectral correction = N_c^{3N_c}/g^g = 3^9/7^7
invariants.geometric_source: k=2 eigenspace correction on D_IV^5

invariants.name: d_2 = N_c^{N_c} = 27
invariants.formula: Second eigenspace degeneracy is a BST power: d_2 = 27 = N_c^N_c = 3^3.
invariants.geometric_source: Hilbert function of Q^5 at k=2

invariants.name: 483473 = N_max * (g^2*C_2^2*rank + 1) = 137 * 3529
invariants.formula: zeta_B(0) numerator: 483473 = N_max * (g^2*C_2^2*rank + 1). Both 137 and 3529 are prime. Same tower architecture: BST_product + small_BST_integer.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1796 correction. Tower factorization of spectral zeta numerator.

invariants.name: 5529600 = 483840 * rank^4 * n_C / g
invariants.formula: zeta_B(-1) denominator extends zeta_B(0) denominator by BST ratio: 5529600 = 483840 * 80/7 = 2^13*3^3*5^2. BST-pure.
invariants.geometric_source: Lyra Toy 1796 correction. Hurwitz regularization at a=7/2.

invariants.name: Scattering matrix S(mu) on D_IV^5
invariants.geometric_source: c(-mu)/c(mu) from Plancherel c-function on D_IV^5

invariants.name: Scattering matrix at Wallach midpoint = C_2
invariants.geometric_source: Scattering matrix evaluated at mu = n_C/rank on D_IV^5

invariants.name: Short-root scattering factor
invariants.formula: S_short(mu) = (mu + N_c/rank) / (N_c/rank - mu) = (mu + 3/2)/(3/2 - mu). Short roots in B_2 have multiplicity m_s = 3.
invariants.geometric_source: Short root contribution to S(mu) on D_IV^5

invariants.name: Long-root scattering factor
invariants.formula: S_long(mu) = (mu + 1/rank) / (1/rank - mu) = (mu + 1/2)/(1/2 - mu). Long roots in B_2 have multiplicity m_l = 1.
invariants.geometric_source: Long root contribution to S(mu) on D_IV^5

invariants.name: Extended Golay code [24,12,8] = [rank^2*C_2, rank*C_2, rank^3]
invariants.formula: n=24=rank^2*C_2, k=12=rank*C_2, d=8=rank^3. Rate = 1/rank. Leech lattice ancestor.
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1794. Code construction from GF(2^(rank*C_2-1)). Self-dual code whose dimension = Leech lattice dimension.
invariants.notes: Toy 1794. Extended Golay(24,12,8) = [rank^2*C_2, rank*C_2, rank^3]. All parameters BST products.

invariants.name: BCH(31,6,15) = [2^n_C-1, C_2, N_c*n_C] over GF(2^n_C)
invariants.formula: n=31=2^n_C-1 (Mersenne), k=6=C_2, d=15=N_c*n_C. Code over the Mersenne field at complex dimension.
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1794. BCH construction over GF(2^n_C)=GF(32). Block length is Mersenne number at n_C.
invariants.notes: Toy 1794. k=C_2 is the code dimension. Mersenne primality at n_C.

invariants.name: BCH(127,64,21) = [2^g-1, 2^C_2, N_c*g] over GF(2^g)
invariants.formula: n=127=2^g-1 (Mersenne prime), k=64=2^C_2, d=21=N_c*g. Code over the function catalog field GF(128)=GF(2^g).
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1794. BCH construction over GF(2^g)=GF(128). Block length is Mersenne prime at g.
invariants.notes: Toy 1794. n=N_max-10, k=2^C_2, d=N_c*g.

invariants.name: C_2 = n_C + 1 = RM(1,n_C) code dimension
invariants.formula: Reed-Muller RM(1,m) has k=m+1 information bits. At m=n_C: k=n_C+1=C_2. The Casimir IS the first-order Reed-Muller dimension at the complex dimension.
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1794. Linear functions in n_C variables have n_C slopes + 1 intercept = C_2 degrees of freedom.
invariants.notes: Toy 1794. Two readings: geometry (Casimir rank) and information theory (RM code dimension). Same identity.

invariants.name: Singleton bound at Hamming(g,rank^2,N_c) = n_C
invariants.formula: k <= n-d+1 = g-N_c+1 = 5 = n_C. Max information rate at distance N_c on g-bit codewords = n_C.
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1794. Singleton bound evaluated at BST Hamming code parameters.
invariants.notes: Toy 1794. The complex dimension n_C is the maximum code rate at confinement distance N_c.

invariants.name: Leech lattice kissing number = rank^4 * N_c * (2^(rank*C_2) - 1)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1794. Leech lattice in dim rank^2*C_2 = 24.
invariants.notes: Toy 1794. E8 kissing = rank^4*N_c*n_C = 240. Leech = rank^4*N_c*(2^(rank*C_2)-1) = 196560.

invariants.name: E8 kissing number = rank^4 * N_c * n_C = 240
invariants.formula: 240 = rank^4 * N_c * n_C = 16 * 3 * 5. E8 lattice in dim rank^3 = 8.
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1794. E8 lattice dimension = rank^3 = 8.
invariants.notes: Toy 1794. rank^4*N_c*n_C = all five integers in the kissing number.

invariants.name: Na/K-ATPase pump stoichiometry = N_c/rank = 3/2
invariants.formula: 3 Na+ out : 2 K+ in = N_c : rank. The fundamental ion pump IS the short root ratio.
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1797. N_c/rank = short root shift = Wallach half-parameter. Biology implements the B_2(3,1) root structure.
invariants.notes: Toy 1797. ATP: N_c phosphates -> rank phosphates. Pump ratio = N_c/rank.

invariants.name: Potassium concentration ratio [K+]_in/[K+]_out = n_C*g = 35
invariants.formula: [K+]_in/[K+]_out = 140/4 = 35 = n_C*g. Exact at standard textbook values.
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1797. Ion gradient maintained by Na/K pump at BST stoichiometry.
invariants.notes: Toy 1797. Depends on textbook reference values (vary by cell type). n_C*g = 35.

invariants.name: DNA B-form base pairs per turn = N_c*g/rank = 21/2 = 10.5

invariants.notes: Toy 1923. Z-6. WHY α is exact: QED is discrete series, not continuous.

invariants.name: EW spectral parameter r₂² = c₂(Q⁵)/rank = 11/2 (continuous)

invariants.notes: Toy 1923. Z-6. Chern class c_2 determines the EW spectral parameter.

invariants.name: Unused spectral levels = N_max - N_c^2 = 128 = 2^g = function catalog
invariants.formula: 137 - 9 = 128 = 2^7. The unused spectrum IS the periodic table of functions.
invariants.notes: Toy 1936. Paper #92. N_max - K_max = 128 = 2^g.

invariants.name: Hamming(g,rank^2,N_c) is PERFECT: 2^g/(1+g) = rank^4 = 16
invariants.formula: 128/8 = 16 = rank^4. The confinement code achieves the Hamming bound.
invariants.notes: Toy 1936. Paper #92. Perfect code → optimal error correction.

invariants.name: Total substrate capacity = rank^5*N_c*N_max = 13152
invariants.notes: Toy 1936. Paper #92. Total information capacity of D_IV^5.

invariants.name: DM/baryon = rank^4/N_c = 16/3 = codewords/color singlets
invariants.formula: 16 valid Hamming codewords, N_c=3 are baryonic → ratio = 16/3 = 5.333.
invariants.notes: Toy 1936. Paper #92. DM = non-baryonic codewords. Mechanism, not fit.

invariants.notes: Toy 1990. SE-7. The heptit: BST native computational primitive with 2.807 bits.

invariants.name: d(1)+1 = rank^3 = 8: the byte is a BST invariant
invariants.formula: d(1)+1 = g+1 = rank^3 = 8 distinguishable states at mass gap
invariants.notes: Toy 1990. SE-7. 8 states = 3 bits = 1 byte. The byte is geometric.

invariants.name: BST native gate set: dim SO(5) = rank*n_C = 10 generators
invariants.notes: Toy 1990. SE-7. SO(5) isometry provides 10 native computation gates for heptit.

invariants.name: Casimir engine max efficiency = n_C/g = 5/7 = 71.4%
invariants.formula: eta_max = n_C/g = 5/7 (BST Casimir heat engine limit)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1990 / Paper #26.
invariants.notes: Toy 1990. SE-8. Casimir flow cell efficiency bound. Paper #26.

invariants.name: Casimir engine optimal stroke = g/rank = 7/2 = 3.5
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1990 / Paper #26.
invariants.notes: Toy 1990. SE-8. Optimal Casimir stroke ratio.

invariants.name: Mass gap error correction = Hamming(7,4,3)
invariants.formula: d(1) = 7 modes, can correct floor((7-1)/2) = 3 errors
invariants.notes: Toy 1990. SE-7. Mass gap has native Hamming(7,4,3) error correction. Same as Toy 1858.

invariants.name: Casimir harvesting efficiency = n_C/g = 5/7 = 71.4%
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1991. Paper #26. Efficiency = second eigenvalue resonance ratio.
invariants.notes: Toy 1991. Casimir efficiency eta = n_C/g = Delta_2/lambda_2. Connects vacuum energy to eigenvalue structure.

invariants.name: Casimir stroke ratio = g/rank = 7/2 = 3.5
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1991. Paper #26.
invariants.notes: Toy 1991. Optimal d_max/d_min for Casimir work extraction.

invariants.name: Lifshitz repulsion fraction = rank/g = 2/7
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1991. Paper #26.
invariants.notes: Toy 1991. Fraction of Casimir force from Lifshitz repulsion in high-eps materials.

invariants.name: Lifshitz correction coefficient = rank^4/N_c = 16/3
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1991/1993. Lifshitz formula F_corr ~ (16/3)*alpha/sqrt(eps).
invariants.notes: Toy 1991/1993. The 16/3 in Lifshitz's finite-eps Casimir correction is rank^4/N_c.

invariants.name: Optimal Casimir stroke = N_c = 3 unit cells (137 to 140 planes)
invariants.formula: N_c = 3 unit cells between N_max and N_max+N_c planes
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1991. Stroke between spectral resonance and rank^2*n_C*g.
invariants.notes: Toy 1991. Color charge N_c is the minimum reliable stroke for Casimir harvesting.

invariants.name: Hamming(g, rank^2, N_c) = (7, 4, 3) perfect error correction
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1992. T1689. Simplest perfect code is entirely BST.
invariants.notes: Toy 1992. Every parameter of Hamming(7,4,3) is a BST integer. Block=g, data=rank^2, distance=N_c.

invariants.name: Reed-Solomon field GF(2^g) = GF(128), block N_max-1 = 136
invariants.notes: Toy 1992. RS over GF(2^g) with block = N_max-1 = rank^3*seesaw.

invariants.name: Error correction overhead = N_c/rank^2 = 3/4 = 75%
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1992. T1689. Physical constant, not design choice.
invariants.notes: Toy 1992. To store rank^2=4 data bits reliably requires N_c=3 parity bits.

invariants.name: Spectral channel capacity = log2(N_max) ~ g = 7 bits
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1992. T1689. N_max distinguishable states per channel.
invariants.notes: Toy 1992. log2(N_max) ~ g because 2^g = 128 ~ N_max = 137. [C→S 2026-05-15 by Grace: log2(137) ≈ g (1.4%). Information-theoretic identification of N_max with g bits — structural.]

invariants.name: Z(Ga) = 2^n_C - 1 = 31 (Mersenne prime)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1992. Gallium = Mersenne prime atomic number.
invariants.notes: Toy 1992. GaAs quantum dots: Z(Ga)=2^n_C-1=31 Mersenne, Z(As)=N_c*c_2=33.

invariants.name: d(4) = rank*g*c_3 = 182 (eigenvalue multiplicity)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1992. Multiplicity at k=4 (Higgs eigenvalue).

invariants.name: d(5) = rank*N_c^3*g = 378 (eigenvalue multiplicity)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1992. Multiplicity at k=5 (top quark eigenvalue).

invariants.name: Bulk modulus BaTiO3 = rank*N_c^(rank^2) = 162 GPa EXACT
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1993. Bulk modulus = BST product.
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. B(BaTiO3) = rank*N_c^4 = 162 GPa. Every elastic property of BaTiO3 is BST.

invariants.name: BaTiO3 orthorhombic-tetragonal transition = rank*N_max + rank^2 = 278 K
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1993. Second phase transition of BaTiO3.
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. All three BaTiO3 phase transitions are BST products.

invariants.name: BaTiO3 rhombohedral-orthorhombic transition = N_max + chern_sum + rank^2 = 183 K
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1993. First phase transition of BaTiO3.
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. T_R_O = N_max + 42 + rank^2.

invariants.name: BaTiO3 spontaneous polarization = rank*c_3 = 26 uC/cm^2
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. P_s = rank*c_3 = rank*(g+C_2) = 26 uC/cm^2.

invariants.name: BaTiO3 coercive field = n_C = 5 kV/cm
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. Switching field of the BST engineering material.

invariants.name: Ba-137 mass number = N_max = 137 (THE BST isotope)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1993. Isotope engineering: Ba-137 TiO3 = maximum spectral alignment.
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. Isotopically pure Ba-137 TiO3 has every atom resonant with spectral cutoff.

invariants.name: Si-28 mass number = rank^2*g = 28 (qubit isotope)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1993. Si-28 has I=0 (zero nuclear spin = no magnetic decoherence).
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. Si-28 = rank^2*g. Used for ultra-long T2 qubits (seconds at 4K).

invariants.name: C-12 mass number = rank^2*N_c = 12 (diamond qubit isotope)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1993. C-12 has I=0. C-13 (c_3=13) has I=1/2 (decoherence source).
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. C-12 diamond: I=0 host for NV center qubits. C-13=c_3 causes decoherence.

invariants.name: Superlattice (8|4) BaTiO3 sublayer = Delta_1 = rank^3 = 8 UC (mass gap filter)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1993. Superlattice as dual-band spectral filter.
invariants.notes: Toy 1993. (8|4) sublayer=Delta_1 (mass gap), period=Delta_3 (electroweak). Dual-band filter.

invariants.name: Casimir pressure at N_max=137 BaTiO3 planes
invariants.notes: Toy 1970. Casimir pressure at BST-optimal gap = BST product.

invariants.name: Piezoelectric boundary condition energy (mV scale)
invariants.notes: Toy 1970. Piezo is cheapest active boundary condition method. [2026-05-15 Grace: I→S: 2V across 55nm BaTiO3 structural, material-specific.]

invariants.name: BaTiO3 Casimir switching ratio = n_C = 5
invariants.notes: Toy 1970. BaTiO3 ferro/para switching ratio.

invariants.name: Strain to shift 1 lattice plane = alpha = 1/N_max
invariants.notes: Toy 1970. 1/137 strain = fine structure constant.

invariants.name: LSCO T_c = rank*(seesaw+rank) = 38 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. La2-xSrxCuO4 optimal T_c.

invariants.name: Hg-1201 T_c ~ base*(1+1/19) = 97 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. 1-layer Hg cuprate. [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, 1-2%]

invariants.name: Hg-1212 T_c = rank^g = 128 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. 2-layer Hg cuprate.

invariants.name: Hg-1223 T_c = N_max - rank^2 = 133 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. 3-layer Hg cuprate. Highest confirmed T_c at ambient P.

invariants.name: Hg-1234 T_c = M_g = 127 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. 4-layer Hg cuprate. Mersenne prime.

invariants.name: Bi-2223 T_c = rank*n_C*c_2 = 110 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. BSCCO family.

invariants.name: Tl-2223 T_c = n_C^3 = 125 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. Thallium cuprate.

invariants.name: Bi-2212 T_c = n_C*seesaw = 85 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. BSCCO 2-layer.

invariants.name: Tl-2212 T_c = rank^2*N_c^3 = 108 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. Tl 2-layer cuprate.

invariants.name: V3Si T_c = seesaw = 17 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. A15 compound.

invariants.name: H3S T_c = g*(rank^2*g+1) = 203 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. At 155 GPa. First room-temp-approach SC.

invariants.name: LaH10 T_c = rank*n_C^3 = 250 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. At 170 GPa.

invariants.name: CSH T_c (claimed) = rank^5*N_c^2 = 288 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. Dias claim (retracted). BST predicted decomposition. [C→S 2026-05-15 by Grace: claimed T_c = rank^5·N_c^2 for some superconductor — speculative prediction.]

invariants.name: 276 K superconductor prediction = rank*(N_max+1)
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. 276 = rank^2*N_c*23 = C_2*rank*23 = 12*23. Layer-3 cuprate.

invariants.name: Optimal CuO2 planes = N_c = 3 (confinement)
invariants.notes: Toy 1974. Same as QCD color singlet saturation.

invariants.name: BST wire diameter = rank*n_C*21 = 210 nm
invariants.notes: Toy 1975. 100nm CuO2 core + 55nm BaTiO3 sheath = 210nm = rank*n_C*N_c*g.

invariants.name: BST wire core = rank^2*n_C^2 = 100 nm
invariants.notes: Toy 1975. CuO2 core with N_c=3 planes.

invariants.name: Ocean floor temperature = n_C^2*c_2 = 275 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1976. Deep ocean = 2 C = n_C^2*c_2 K. 1K below BST SC prediction.

invariants.name: Solar surface temperature = C_2*(g*N_max+rank^2) = 5778 K
invariants.formula: C_2*(g*N_max+rank^2) = chern_sum*N_max + rank^2*C_2 = 42*137+24 = 5778 K (two equivalent BST readings)
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1976 + Toy 2391 (Elie). Primary: C_2*(g*N_max+rank^2) = 6*963. Alternative: chern_sum*N_max + rank^2*C_2 = total Chern × spectral cap + rank²·C_2. Both readings exact. Connects T1678 (original) + T1920 (Chern-flux family) via chern_sum=42.
invariants.notes: Toy 1976 + Toy 2391 cross-verification. Two equivalent BST forms strengthen the entry. chern_sum = 42 connection ties to T1920 Chern-flux box-diagram identity.

invariants.name: QCD phase transition = rank^2*N_c*c_3 = 156 MeV
invariants.notes: Toy 1976. QCD crossover T_c. MeV. [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, <1%]

invariants.name: EW phase transition = rank^5*n_C = 160 GeV
invariants.notes: Toy 1976. Electroweak symmetry breaking. GeV. [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, <1%]

invariants.name: Earth albedo = N_c/(rank*n_C) = 0.3
invariants.notes: Toy 1976. Same as Poisson ratio = 3/10.

invariants.name: BaTiO3 piezo d33 = rank*n_C*19 = 190 pC/N
invariants.notes: Toy 1976. BaTiO3 piezoelectric coefficient.

invariants.name: Niobium Debye temperature = n_C^2*c_2 = 275 K = T_ocean
invariants.notes: Toy 1976. Nb Debye = ocean floor temp. Same BST product.

invariants.name: YBCO penetration depth lambda_ab = rank*N_c*n_C^2 = 150 nm
invariants.notes: Toy 1976/1985. London penetration depth in nm.

invariants.name: Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes = rank*n_C = 10
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. Tenfold way = rank*n_C.

invariants.name: Aluminum T_c = C_2/n_C = 6/5 = 1.2 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. Transmon qubit SC.

invariants.name: Cooper pair = rank = 2 electrons
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. Pairing = rank.

invariants.name: IBM Eagle qubits = M_g = 127
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. IBM Eagle chip qubit count = Mersenne prime.

invariants.name: QEC threshold = 1/(rank^2*n_C^2) = 1% = 0.01
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. Surface code threshold.

invariants.name: Transmon T2/T1 ~ N_c/n_C = 0.6
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. Typical transmon ratio.

invariants.name: Bi2Se3 topological gap = N_c/(rank*n_C) = 0.3 eV
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. Canonical topological insulator.

invariants.name: Si-28 T2 = rank^2*n_C*N_c^2*c_3 = 2340 s
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. 39 minutes quantum memory. Each factor traceable.

invariants.name: P donor ionization in Si ~ N_c^2*n_C = 45 meV
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. Key donor for Si qubits. [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, 1-2%]

invariants.name: Diamond NV T2/T1 = N_c/(rank*n_C) = 0.30
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. T2~1ms, T1~3ms at RT.

invariants.name: FeTeSe T_c = rank*g = 14 K
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. Topological SC candidate.

invariants.name: SiC band gap ~ N_c + 1/rank^2 = 3.25 eV (4H)
invariants.notes: Toy 1983. 4H-SiC polytype. [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, <1%]

invariants.name: Mu-metal relative permeability = rank^g*n_C^(rank^2) = 80000
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. 2^7 * 5^4 = 128 * 625 = 80000. World most important magnetic shield.

invariants.name: Neodymium Z = rank^2*N_c*n_C = 60
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. NdFeB permanent magnet.

invariants.name: NdFeB atoms/cell = seesaw = 17
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. Nd2Fe14B = 2+14+1 = 17. Seesaw number.

invariants.name: NdFeB BH_max ~ rank^4*n_C^2 = 400 kJ/m^3
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. Maximum energy product.

invariants.name: Mn12 molecular magnet spin S = rank*n_C = 10
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. Largest known SMM ground spin.

invariants.name: SMM blocking temperature ~ N_c = 3 K (Mn12)
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. Current best SMM blocks at N_c K.

invariants.name: SrTiO3 dielectric constant (4K) ~ rank^3*n_C^5 = 25000
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. Quantum paraelectric. 8*3125.

invariants.name: YBCO upper critical field B_c2(77K) ~ g = 7 T
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. Perfect Meissner shielding up to genus Tesla.

invariants.name: BST supermagnetic prediction: Dy17 cluster, 17=seesaw centers
invariants.formula: seesaw metal centers with S=rank*n_C per ion
invariants.notes: Toy 1985. Room-temp persistent magnetization target: Dy17 cluster.

invariants.name: Cu bulk modulus = N_max = 137 GPa
invariants.formula: K(Cu) = 137 GPa = N_max GPa. Mass=N_c^2*g, Debye=g^3 → involves g^g.
invariants.notes: SE-10. Cu stiffness = fine structure constant in GPa.

invariants.name: BaTiO₃ couples through 5 BST channels simultaneously
invariants.formula: Ferroelectric switching=n_C, Poisson=3/10, ABO3=N_c atoms, 137 planes=N_max, phase transition.
invariants.notes: SE-9. Optimal spectral antenna material.

invariants.name: Bi-2223 T_c = rank*n_C*(rank*n_C+1) = 110 K
invariants.notes: T1676. 11 = rank*n_C+1 = dimensional complement.

invariants.name: Tl-2223 T_c = n_C^3 = 125 K
invariants.formula: 125 = n_C^3 = 5^3. Pure cube of complex dimension.
invariants.notes: T1676. Tl-2223 = n_C cubed.

invariants.name: LaH₁₀ T_c = rank*n_C^3 = 250 K (under pressure)
invariants.notes: T1676. Hydride. rank*n_C^3 at 170 GPa.

invariants.name: Hg-1223 T_c = g*19 = 133 K (highest ambient cuprate)
invariants.notes: T1676. Highest ambient-pressure T_c. g*(rank*N_c^2+1).

invariants.name: g^g = 7^7 = 823543 appears in Cu mass*Debye^2 product
invariants.notes: SE-15. Self-referential: genus raised to itself.

invariants.name: N_max = K_max*lambda_2 + c_2 = 9*14 + 11 = 137
invariants.formula: N_max = N_c^2*(rank*g) + c_2(Q^5). Spectral cap = discrete*second + Chern.
invariants.notes: SE-6.4. NEW identity: 137 = 9*14 + 11.

invariants.name: Diamond NV: N replaces C = g replaces C_2, difference = 1 = unit gap
invariants.formula: Z(N)=7=g, Z(C)=6=C_2. The NV defect IS the BST unit gap g-C_2=1.
invariants.notes: T1673. WHY NV centers work: the defect is the unit gap.

invariants.name: ALL 10 tested spin-0 isotopes have BST-product mass numbers
invariants.formula: C-12=rank*C_2, O-16=rank^4, Si-28=rank^2*g, Ca-40=rank^3*n_C, Fe-56=rank^3*g, Pb-208=rank^4*13, etc.
invariants.notes: T1674. BST selects which nuclei don't cause decoherence.

invariants.name: P-31 qubit donor = 2^n_C - 1 = 31 (Mersenne prime at n_C)
invariants.formula: 31 = 2^5 - 1 = Mersenne prime at n_C. Same as BCH(31,6,15) block length.
invariants.notes: T1674. The optimal qubit donor IS a Mersenne prime.

invariants.name: Si-28 coherence time = 39 min = N_c*13 = N_c*(g+C_2)
invariants.formula: 39 = N_c*(g+C_2) = 3*13. Same BST product as MgB2 T_c.
invariants.notes: T1673. Coherence time = Thirteen × color. Same as MgB2.

invariants.name: Cheeger topological qubit error = exp(-N_c*g) = exp(-21) ≈ 10^-9
invariants.formula: Below surface code threshold without error correction. Wire = g*xi_0.
invariants.notes: T1673. exp(-N_c*g) < 10^-3 threshold. No EC needed.

invariants.name: Proton Bekenstein bound = C_2*g = 42 bits
invariants.formula: Proton stores C_2*g = 42 bits from Hamming(7,4,3): C_2=6 slots × g=7 modes/slot. NOT from Bekenstein (which gives 36 bits at charge radius). Information radius = g/C_2 × charge radius = 0.975 fm.
invariants.notes: CORRECTED. 42 = Hamming counting, not Bekenstein bound. Bekenstein gives 36 at charge radius. R_info/R_charge = g/C_2 = 7/6.

invariants.name: Total spectral parallelism = sum P(k) for k=0..9 = 8008
invariants.formula: Sum of Hilbert function P(k) for k=0 through K_max=N_c^2=9.
invariants.notes: T1675. 8008 parallel computation channels on D_IV^5.

invariants.name: Mass gap register: d(1)+1 = g+1 = rank^3 = 8 states = 1 byte
invariants.formula: g=7 degenerate states + 1 vacuum = 8 = rank^3 = 1 byte.
invariants.geometric_source: Toy 1987 / Lyra Toy 1990.
invariants.notes: T1684. The proton IS an 8-state register. g+1=rank^3.

invariants.name: Mu-metal permeability = rank^g * n_C^(rank^2) = 2^7 * 5^4 = 80,000
invariants.formula: mu_r = 128*625 = 80000. Self-referential: g as exponent of rank, rank^2 as exponent of n_C.
invariants.geometric_source: Keeper.
invariants.notes: T1679. Magnetic shielding = BST self-referential product.

invariants.name: BaTiO₃/SrTiO₃ (8|4) = (rank^3|rank^2) unique BST-optimal superlattice
invariants.geometric_source: Elie Toy 1978.
invariants.notes: T1688. N_max-1=rank^3*seesaw=136 active planes.

invariants.name: T2 enhancement at N_max cavity planes: factor = rank = 2
invariants.notes: Toy 1994. SE-3. PREDICTION: Diamond NV T2 doubles in N_max-plane Casimir cavity. [C→S 2026-05-15 by Grace: NV center T2 enhancement structural ratio identification.]

invariants.name: Aluminum Z = c_3 = 13 (third Chern class)
invariants.notes: Toy 1994. SE-3. Aluminum (transmon material) has Z = third Chern class. [RETRO-2: upgraded I→D, T1783 Chern sum uniqueness provides mechanism] [Re-attributed 2026-05-15 by Grace per Casey directive: theorem field T186 was a batch-upgrade default; actual load-bearing mechanism is T1783 (Chern sum / Chern class). Tier unchanged.]

invariants.name: Aluminum A = N_c^3 = 27 (mass number = color cube)
invariants.notes: Toy 1994. SE-3. Aluminum-27 = N_c^3 = d(2). Transmon material encodes color cube. [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, <1%]

invariants.name: Al2O3 formula weight = rank*N_c*seesaw = 102
invariants.notes: Toy 1994. SE-3. Alumina (transmon barrier) weight = BST product.

invariants.name: BaTiO3 soft mode at omega/omega_D = 1/(rank*n_C) = 1/10
invariants.notes: Toy 1994. SE-1/SE-5. Soft mode frequency is BST fraction of Debye cutoff. [Reclassified I→S 2026-05-15 by Grace. Material-specific ferroelectric soft mode. 1/(rank·n_C) = 0.10 structural identification, not derivation.]

invariants.name: Trit-cube register: (g+1)*(N_c^3+1) = rank^5*g = 224 states
invariants.notes: Toy 1995. SE-7. Two-level substrate register has rank^5*g = 224 states.

invariants.name: Total substrate gate count = c_2 = 11 (SO(5) x SO(2))
invariants.formula: dim SO(5) + dim SO(2) = 10+1 = c_2 = 11 generators
invariants.notes: Toy 1995. SE-7. Native gate set has c_2 = second Chern class generators.

invariants.name: Gate depth per error correction cycle = N_c*n_C = 15
invariants.notes: Toy 1995. SE-7. Maximum gate depth before error correction = N_c*n_C.

invariants.name: Spectral multiplexing: 375 = (n_C/rank)*Q_3 channels
invariants.notes: Toy 1995. SE-7. FE duality bridge supports 375 multiplexed channels.

invariants.name: First BST photonic sideband ratio = n_C/rank^2 = 5/4
invariants.notes: Toy 1996. SE-5. Photonic crystal sideband spacing.

invariants.name: Cu/Si acoustic impedance ratio ~ rank + 1/C_2 = 13/6
invariants.notes: Toy 1996. SE-5. Phononic crystal impedance mismatch is BST fraction. [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, <1%]

invariants.name: BST negative index = -sqrt(n_C/N_c) = -sqrt(5/3)
invariants.notes: Toy 1996. SE-5. Negative index = sqrt of Kolmogorov exponent.

invariants.name: BST hyperbolic metamaterial fill fraction = rank/g = 2/7
invariants.notes: Toy 1996. SE-5. Lifshitz repulsion fraction = metal fill for hyperbolics.

invariants.name: Hyperbolic resolution enhancement = rank*n_C = 10x
invariants.notes: Toy 1996. SE-5. Sub-diffraction imaging at rank*n_C = 10x.

invariants.name: Off-resonance Casimir with metasurface = N_c/n_C = 3/5
invariants.notes: Toy 1996. SE-8. Metasurface reduces Casimir to N_c/n_C off resonance.

invariants.name: BST phase gate = 4pi/g = 4pi/7 per period
invariants.formula: Delta_phi = 2*pi*rank/g = 4*pi/7. Z_g cyclic after g periods.
invariants.notes: Toy 1996. SE-7. Photonic phase gate is Z_g cyclic with period g=7.

invariants.name: Proton windings per macro-bit = rank^2*(rank*n_C+1) = 44
invariants.notes: Toy 1997. Each of 42 macro-bits = 44 windings on S^2×S^1.

invariants.name: Al atomic mass = N_c^N_c = 27 amu (color to the color)
invariants.formula: 27 = N_c^N_c = N_c^3 = P(2). Self-referential: color raised to itself.
invariants.notes: Toy 1998. SE-15. Also = P(2) = second eigenvalue multiplicity.

invariants.name: N_c^N_c = P(2) = 27: self-referential power = eigenvalue multiplicity
invariants.notes: Toy 1998. The multiplicity of the second eigenvalue IS color^color.

invariants.name: a(BaTiO₃)/a(SrTiO₃) = c₂*g/(N_c*n_C²) = 77/75
invariants.notes: Toy 1999. BTO/STO lattice ratio = Chern*genus / (color*dim^2).

invariants.name: 17 superlattice periods × rank³ BTO = N_max-1 = 136 planes
invariants.formula: 17*8 = 136 = N_max-1. Seesaw × rank³ = spectral cap minus 1.
invariants.notes: Toy 1999. The optimal BTO/STO superlattice has N_max-1 active BTO planes.

invariants.name: Casimir optimal gap = N_max lattice planes = 54.9 nm

invariants.name: c-function weight leading exponent on wall
invariants.geometric_source: Short root multiplicity m_s = N_c = 3
invariants.notes: Wall Plancherel weight vanishes as t^5. Suppresses negative digamma contributions near t=0.

invariants.name: Hadamard positivity margin at safe evaluation point
invariants.geometric_source: Safe point Re = n_C/2 + 1 = 7/2, distance to critical strip = 5/2
invariants.notes: Every Hadamard term at Re=7/2 has Re > 0 with margin >= 5/2. I_safe > 0 unconditionally.

invariants.name: Total elliptic curves tested for BSD transfer chain
invariants.geometric_source: Toys 1415+2085+2086: ranks 0-5, conductors up to 234446
invariants.notes: Zero exceptions. Square system topological, rank-independent.

invariants.name: Scattering matrix shift from short root multiplicity
invariants.geometric_source: B_2 root system, Langlands-Shahidi functional equation
invariants.notes: m_2(s) = xi(s-2)/xi(s+1). Places critical line at s = n_C/2 - (N_c-1) = 1/2.

invariants.name: Over-determination ratio for BST integers from Hodge constraints
invariants.geometric_source: 33 constraints from 6 disciplines across 5 integers
invariants.notes: Each integer pinned by min 6 independent constraints. Removing any one leaves integer still determined.

invariants.name: Number of rank-2 BSDs surviving 8 Hodge filters
invariants.geometric_source: 32 candidates, 8 filters, 1 survivor
invariants.notes: F4 (Selberg d_F<=2) + F6 (m_s>=3) algebraically squeeze n to exactly 5.

invariants.name: Sum of Chern classes of Q^5
invariants.geometric_source: Chern ring of compact dual Q^5 = SO(7)/[SO(5)xSO(2)]
invariants.notes: Total Chern = 42 = C_2*g including c_0=1. Without c_0: 41.

invariants.name: Hodge level of Horikawa surface (h^{2,0}=2)
invariants.geometric_source: Horikawa surface K^2=1, p_g=2
invariants.notes: h^{2,0}>=2 falls outside Type IV domain. Period domain is Type III. BST framework structurally inapplicable. This IS the ~85% Hodge boundary.

invariants.name: Chern sum of Q^5 = 42 (unique among all Q^n)
invariants.geometric_source: Chern ring of Q^n = SO(n+2)/[SO(n)xSO(2)]
invariants.notes: S(Q^n) = 2,5,10,21,42,85,170,341... strictly increasing ~2^n/3. 42 = C_2*g occurs only at n=5. Independent topological filter.

invariants.name: Chern sum sequence S(Q^n) for n=1..10
invariants.geometric_source: Total Chern classes of quadric Q^n
invariants.notes: S(Q^4)=21=3*7, S(Q^5)=42=2*3*7, S(Q^10)=1365=3*5*7*13. BST integers 3,7 appear in multiple sums but C_2*g=42 only at n=5.

invariants.name: Non-orthogonal rank-2 BSDs eliminated by F1
invariants.geometric_source: Root system filter: requires B_n type
invariants.notes: Exclusion Lemma 4.1. Non-orthogonal domains lack short root multiplicity.

invariants.name: Even-dimensional Type IV domains eliminated by F2
invariants.geometric_source: Tube domain condition
invariants.notes: Exclusion Lemma 4.2.

invariants.name: Type IV with n>=7 eliminated by F4 (Selberg degree)
invariants.geometric_source: Selberg degree bound from L-function Gamma factors
invariants.notes: Exclusion Lemma 4.3. Combined with F6 (m_s>=3 kills n=3), forces n=5.

invariants.name: F4+F6 algebraic squeeze: n must equal exactly 5
invariants.formula: F4: n <= 5 (Selberg), F6: n >= 5 (m_s >= 3). Intersection: n = 5.
invariants.geometric_source: Hodge-theoretic constraints on Type IV domains
invariants.notes: Primary uniqueness mechanism. F4 (Selberg d_F<=2) forces n<=5. F6 (m_s>=3 for c-function suppression) forces n>=5. Only n=5 survives.

invariants.name: First eigenvalue of 2-form Laplacian on Q^5
invariants.geometric_source: Bochner-Weitzenbock identity on compact dual Q^5
invariants.notes: The 2-form spectral gap is c_2 = 11 (second Chern class). Determines adjoint-sector (pure gauge) mass gap. Key identity: c_2 = C_2 + n_C.

invariants.name: Glueball 0++ mass from 2-form spectral gap (D-tier)
invariants.geometric_source: 2-form Laplacian gap c_2=11 on Q^5 via Weitzenbock
invariants.notes: Tier upgraded from I to D via YM-6 Weitzenbock completion. Lattice: 1710 +/- 50 MeV (Morningstar-Peardon 1999). Same pi^5*m_e formula as proton but with c_2 replacing C_2.

invariants.name: Pure-gauge beta_0 = 11 = c_2 (Cal's identification)
invariants.geometric_source: QCD one-loop beta function at n_f=0 matches second Chern class
invariants.notes: Cal's observation: pure-gauge beta_0 = 11 = c_2, not beta_0 = 7 = g (which is SM with n_f=6). Topological-physical match: Chern class = running coupling coefficient.

invariants.name: SM beta_0 = 7 = g (with n_f = 6 quark flavors)
invariants.geometric_source: QCD one-loop beta function at n_f=6 (full SM)
invariants.notes: Physical SM: beta_0 = 11*N_c/3 - 2*n_f/3 = 11 - 4 = 7 = g. Both beta_0 values (pure and SM) are BST integers: c_2=11 and g=7.

invariants.name: Wightman axioms W1-W5 verification for YM sprint construction
invariants.geometric_source: Keeper YM-8 audit against T1788+T1790
invariants.notes: W3 strengthened: adjoint gap c_2=11 > scalar gap C_2=6. Modular localization extends to 2-form bundle.

invariants.name: Sectional curvature of D_IV^5 (Bergman metric)
invariants.geometric_source: Bergman metric on D_IV^5 = SO_0(5,2)/[SO(5)xSO(2)]
invariants.notes: Negative curvature provides the geometric scale for mass gap. R^4 (K=0) has no such scale. The Curvature Principle: gap requires K != 0.

invariants.name: Spectral gap of D_IV^{N_c+2} for SU(N_c) gauge theory
invariants.geometric_source: Bergman Laplacian on D_IV^{N_c+2}, Toy 1388
invariants.notes: The cross-SU(N) mass ratio uses g (genus), not C_2 (Casimir). m(SU(4))/m(SU(3)) = sqrt(8/7) = 1.069. Lyra's fix: lambda_1 = g = n_C+2 = N_c+4 for the SU(N_c) domain.

invariants.name: F_1 Eichler-Shimura collapse: both sides = rank = 2
invariants.geometric_source: Lefschetz trace at absolute point q=1
invariants.notes: At F_1, Frobenius = identity, T_1 = 2*id. Modularity is a tautology. The arithmetic/analytic distinction has not yet occurred.

invariants.name: Poisson kernel symmetry K(z,xi) = K(xi,z) on D_IV^5
invariants.geometric_source: Hua 1963, Poisson kernel on type IV domains
invariants.notes: Modularity = kernel symmetry. AC(0) depth 0. Self-adjointness means boundary->interior and interior->boundary are adjoint maps.

invariants.name: Interior/boundary dimension ratio = rank = 2
invariants.geometric_source: D_IV^5 real dim 10, Shilov S^4 x S^1 real dim 5
invariants.notes: The interior has exactly rank times the dimension of the boundary. This ratio = rank is structural.

invariants.name: BSD + Sym^2 pole fraction sum = spectral coverage
invariants.geometric_source: L(E,1)/Omega = 1/rank (BSD) + Res(Sym^2,s=1) pole fraction 1/N_c
invariants.notes: New identity from Bloch-Kato computation: 1/2 + 1/3 = 5/6. The BSD ratio and Sym^2 pole fraction sum to n_C/C_2 = spectral coverage ratio on D_IV^5.

invariants.name: Partition-Chern identity: p(C_2) = c_2 = C_2 + n_C = 11
invariants.geometric_source: Number of integer partitions of C_2 equals second Chern number of compact dual Q^5. Equals number of Arthur particle types for Sp(C_2). Equals dim K = dim SO(n_C) + dim SO(rank) = 10 + 1.
invariants.notes: Key identity connecting combinatorics (partitions), geometry (Chern), and representation theory (Arthur). Toy 2164/2165.

invariants.name: Partition function at BST integers: p(2)=2, p(3)=3, p(4)=5, p(5)=7, p(6)=11
invariants.geometric_source: Integer partition function evaluated at BST integers returns BST integers or BST-derived values. Chain: 2,3,5,7,11.
invariants.notes: The partition function reads the BST catalog. Toy 2165.

invariants.name: Symplectic orbits of Sp(C_2): 8 = 2^N_c
invariants.geometric_source: Partitions of C_2=6 with odd parts having even multiplicity. Specific to C_2=6 (not general for Sp(2n)).
invariants.notes: Observed for Sp(C_2) specifically, not a general Sp(2n) formula. Toy 2165. [RETRO-2 batch upgrade: mechanism chain D-tier, <1%]

invariants.name: Symmetric power chain terminates at k=C_2: GL(rank) -> GL(N_c) -> ... -> GL(g)
invariants.formula: Sym^k(49a1) -> GL(k+1) targets: rank, N_c, rank^2, n_C, C_2, g for k=1..C_2
invariants.geometric_source: Functoriality chain from Gelbart-Jacquet through Kim-Shahidi to GRS descent. Terminates at GL(g)=GL(7) via L-group saturation Sp(C_2).
invariants.notes: Three termination reasons: catalog exhaustion, L-group Sp(C_2) saturation, self-duality parity. Toy 2162.

invariants.name: Total A-packet representations for Sp(C_2): 2^(rank^2) = 16
invariants.geometric_source: Sum of component group sizes over all 8 symplectic orbits of Sp(C_2). Component group = (Z/2Z)^a, a = number of distinct even parts.

invariants.name: QUE Weyl remainder exponent on D_IV^5
invariants.formula: N(T) = c_0*T^n_C + O(T^(n_C-1)); remainder exp = n_C-1 = rank^2 = 4

Empirical confirmation that the Pell skeleton has perfect discrimination below p = 200, with controlled leakage beyond from larger Pell half-companion primes. This sharpens the four-skeleton conjecture into a one-skeleton operational filter for Ogg-prime membership in a bounded range.]
